content.problemStatement:
Chef and Chefu are working as gold miners. There are a total of NNN gold mines, numbered 111 through NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th gold mine contains GiG_iGi​ gold in total; if only Chef worked in it, it would take him AiA_iAi​ days to completely mine it, while if only Chefu worked in it, it would take him BiB_iBi​ days.

Each of our miners may only work in one mine at a time, but they may decide to start working in another mine at any time (even in the middle of some day), any number of times. They also choose the mines to work in independently from each other and they may work in the same mine at the same time. Mining gold is a continuous process, i.e. if a miner works for ttt days (where ttt is a real number) in a mine where this miner has mining speed ggg gold per day, then he will mine g⋅tg \cdot tg⋅t gold. Obviously, it is impossible to work in a mine after no gold remains in it. For example, if a gold mine contains 303030 gold and Chef needs 222 days to completely mine it, but he spends 111 day in it, then he will mine 151515 gold; if Chefu needs 111 day to completely mine the same gold mine, and both Chef and Chefu start working in this mine at the same time, it will be empty after 2/32/32/3 days ― Chefu will mine 202020 gold, while Chef will mine 101010 gold.

At each point of time, both Chef and Chefu know the gold mine in which the other miner is working. Each of them wants to gather the maximum amount of gold for himself. Find the amounts of gold the miners will have if they both act optimally.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers GiG_iGi​, AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing two space-separated real numbers ― the amount of gold mined by Chef and the amount of gold mined by Chefu. Your answer will be considered correct if the absolute or relative error of each amount of gold does not exceed 10−610^{-6}10−6.
